response:
Short answer — we tightened scoping and added explicit ownership filtering.

Details:
- SyncEventGrid no longer scopes purely by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks.  
- It now scopes to Rackspace‑managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions -> lighthouse_delegations and requiring:
  - managed_by_tenant_id == Rackspace Lighthouse tenant, and
  - delegation_state in [active, succeeded].
- The sync still uses a single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and deliberately does NOT acquire per‑customer tenant tokens.
- Before upsert, Event Grid rows are filtered by a shared ownership classifier:
  - event subscription name must follow Rackspace naming (rax-/rackspace-), and
  - destination must match Rackspace‑managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance‑v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr‑fleet/compliance resource IDs).
- Sync stats were updated to distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing (SubscriptionNotFound) warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments.
- GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries were aligned to use the same managed‑monitoring scope so KPIs reflect only Rackspace‑managed monitoring subscriptions.
context_injected:
{"ns": "azure-msp", "task": "compliance", "facts": ["azr-fleet-platform uses Event Grid"], "recent": ["2026-04-08: On 2026-04-08 in azr-compliance-v3, implemented Event Grid inventory scoping and Rackspace ownership filtering. SyncEventGrid no longer scopes by customer_subscriptions + monitoring service blocks alone; it now scopes to managed monitoring subscriptions by joining customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations, requiring managed_by_tenant_id to equal the Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in active/succeeded. The sync still uses the single Rackspace Lighthouse credential and intentionally does not acquire per-customer tenant tokens. Event Grid rows are now filtered before upsert by a shared ownership classifier: event subscription name must match Rackspace naming conventions (rax-/rackspace-) and destination must match Rackspace-managed webhook/resource patterns (rackspace.com, compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts, or known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs). Sync stats now distinguish skipped unmanaged subscriptions, stale/missing subscriptions from SubscriptionNotFound warnings, and filtered unmanaged deployments. GetEventGridDeployments summary and list queries now use the same managed monitoring scope so page KPIs reflect only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscript
[specifics: environment: azr-compliance-v3; sync_component: SyncEventGrid; scoping_rule: join customer_subscriptions to lighthouse_delegations requiring managed_by_tenant_id = Rackspace Lighthouse tenant and delegation_state in [active,succeeded]; credential_usage: single Rackspace Lighthouse credential (no per-customer tenant tokens); event_subscription_name_pattern: rax-/rackspace-; destination_patterns: rackspace.com; compliance-v3 azurewebsites hosts; known azr-fleet/compliance resource IDs; GetEventGridDeployments_scope: summary and list use same managed monitoring scope (only Rackspace-managed monitoring subscriptions); sync_stats_categories: skipped_unmanaged_subscriptions; stale_or_missing (SubscriptionNotFound warnings); filtered_unmanaged_deployments]", "2026-04-09: For azr-compliance-v3 PR #72 on 2026-04-09, two live GitHub review threads on GetEventGridDeployments were valid and fixed in commit 633573dc on develop. Fixes: (1) summary query no longer prepends an extra WITH before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE, preventing invalid SQL 'WITH WITH ...'; (2) selected tenant_id now uses COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id) to match join/filter fallback behavior. Added unit assertions to catch both regressions, reran ruff and focused pytest, replied to both threads, and resolved them.
[specifics: repository: azr-compliance-v3; pr_number: 72; commit: 633573dc; branch: develop; function: GetEventGridDeployments; sql_cte: EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE; sql_error: 'WITH WITH' caused by extra WITH prepended before EVENT_GRID_EFFECTIVE_SCOPE_CTE; tenant_id_coalesce: COALESCE(eegd.tenant_id, mms.tenant_id, cs.tenant_id); tests: added unit assertions; reran ruff and focused pytest]", "2026-04-09: Implemented dedicated server-side Event Grid bulk remediation in azr-compliance-v3. Design choice: do not reuse sync_queue; instead use Postgres-backed tables event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs and event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items plus a dedicated 15-second timer worker ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation guarded by its own advisory lock. Bulk start endpoint reuses an active queued/running run, snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL, and persists latest-run item statuses for page refresh continuity. EventGridDetails now calls POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk once, polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges latest item statuses into visible deployable cards, and shows a Latest Bulk Remediation panel.
[specifics: application: azr-compliance-v3; postgres_tables: event_grid_bulk_remediation_runs, event_grid_bulk_remediation_run_items; worker: ProcessEventGridBulkRemediation (15s timer, uses its own advisory lock); endpoints: POST /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk, GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest; sync_queue_reuse: design choice = do not reuse sync_queue; bulk_start_behavior: reuses an active queued/running run and snapshots the full deployable subscription set from shared Event Grid coverage SQL; status_persistence: latest-run item statuses persisted for page-refresh continuity; EventGridDetails_client: issues POST once then polls GET /api/event-grid/remediation/bulk/latest, merges item statuses into deployable cards and shows Latest Bulk Remediation panel]", "2026-04-09: On 2026-04-09 in azr-compliance-v3, integrated Event Grid remediation on the Event Grid Details page as a browser-side Fleet Platform call rather than a backend proxy because the browser reliably has the operator MSAL Bearer token while the backend, behind APIM, may not retain the raw Authorization credential in deployed environments. The page now uses metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi), requests an optional Fleet-specific scope via VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE, calls POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL, polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus, treats succeeded/already_exists as success, maps 404 to not_found, and maps 409 to not_eligible. UI adds per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges.
[specifics: app: azr-compliance-v3; remediation_call: browser-side Fleet Platform call (instead of backend proxy); auth_reason: browser has operator MSAL Bearer token; backend behind APIM may not retain raw Authorization credential; metadata_field: metadata.coverage.customer_id (aliased from ddi); env_var_scope: VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_SCOPE (optional Fleet-specific scope requested); api_call: POST /v1/customers/{customerId}/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/eventgrid/subscription against VITE_FLEET_PLATFORM_API_BASE_URL; polling_mechanism: polls statusUrl until terminal runtimeStatus; success_conditions: treats succeeded and already_exists as success; error_mappings: 404 -> not_found; 409 -> not_eligible; ui_changes: per-subscription Ensure Event Grid and Ensure All Deployable actions plus local remediation state badges]", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in azr-fleet-platform PR #67, three additional Event Grid review findings were validated and fixed in commit 5cd48c3. Fixes: targeted Event Grid DELETE now validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting the delete orchestration while still skipping monitoring-tier eligibility; ensure_event_subscription_single now writes a Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event before returning a customer/subscription mismatch failure; orchestrate_onboarding changed event_grid_result['skipped'] back to a boolean and added skipped_no_monitoring as the explicit count, with finalize_run updated to carry both fields. Targeted Event Grid tests passed and all new review threads were replied to and resolved.
[specifics: repo_pr: azr-fleet-platform #67; commit_hash: 5cd48c3; targeted_event_grid_delete: validates that the subscription belongs to the URL customer before starting delete orchestration; monitoring_tier_eligibility: skipped during targeted Event Grid DELETE; ensure_event_subscription_single: writes Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch event on customer/subscription mismatch; event_name: Cosmos EventGridSubscriptionCustomerMismatch; orchestrate_onboarding: event_grid_result['skipped'] reverted to boolean and added skipped_no_monitoring as explicit count; finalize_run: updated to carry both 'skipped' and 'skipped_no_monitoring' fields; tests: targeted Event Grid tests passed]", "2026-03-23: On 2026-03-23 in fleet-platform-app, Event Grid eligibility was tightened so Event Grid subscriptions are only created/maintained for monitoring-entitled service blocks. Changes were made locally in source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, and OpenAPI docs. Customer-wide Event Grid orchestration now uses only Lighthouse subscriptions with computed_service_tier='advanced'; ensure_event_subscription_single now skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring subscriptions; the targeted Event Grid API returns 409 for subscriptions without monitoring entitlement; onboarding foundation state now marks non-monitoring subscriptions as Event Grid skipped rather than failed. Validation passed with pytest on test_eventgrid_helpers.py and test_http_eventgrid.py plus pre-commit and validate-openapi-spec.
[specifics: app: fleet-platform-app; customer_wide_eventgrid_scope: Lighthouse subscriptions only (computed_service_tier=advanced); ensure_event_subscription_single: skips non-lighthouse or non-monitoring subscriptions; targeted_eventgrid_api_response: HTTP 409 returned for subscriptions without monitoring entitlement; onboarding_foundation_state: non-monitoring subscriptions marked as Event Grid skipped instead of failed; modified_components: source_data, eventgrid activity/orchestrators, onboarding, validation, http_eventgrid, OpenAPI docs; tests_run: pytest on test_eventgrid_helpers.py and test_http_eventgrid.py; validation_tools: pre-commit and validate-openapi-spec]", "2026-03-27: On 2026-03-27 in fleet-platform-app, investigated why global Event Grid filters were not excluding Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces in prod. Live Azure event subscription rax-resource-change-1193525 on subscription 025d123f-b6ac-470a-a420-bf4d3087670f had advanced filters on key data.resourceInfo.type, but a captured raw EventGridSchema ResourceWriteSuccess payload showed subject and data.resourceUri fields, not data.resourceInfo.type. This made the filter ineffective even though it was deployed. Patched functions/shared/eventgrid_filters.py to use Subject-based StringNotContains filters with resource-ID path fragments (for example /providers/Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces/), updated focused tests, and verified Event Grid helper/http/drift tests passed. Existing prod subscriptions will still need Event Grid update/remediation to pick up the corrected filters.
[specifics: app: fleet-platform-app; event_subscription: rax-resource-change-1193525; subscription_id: 025d123f-b6ac-470a-a420-bf4d3087670f; original_filter_field: data.resourceInfo.type; observed_payload_fields: subject, data.resourceUri; patched_file: functions/shared/eventgrid_filters.py; new_filter_type: Subject-based StringNotContains; excluded_fragment: /providers/Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces/; event_schema: ResourceWriteSuccess; tests_verified: focused tests and Event Grid helper/http/drift tests passed]", "2026-03-20: Implemented customer/subscription-specific Event Grid filter override support in fleet-platform-app on 2026-03-20. Added Postgres table eventgrid_filter_overrides via migration 034 and schema sync, PgStore methods resolve_eventgrid_filter_override and upsert_eventgrid_filter_override, global default filters for Event Grid subscriptions, override resolution precedence subscription > customer > global default in functions/activities/eventgrid.py, eventTypes parameter support in the ARM event subscription template, and token-rotation preservation of eventTypes/advancedFilters in functions/triggers/http_eventgrid.py. Added unit tests for Event Grid helper override application and PgStore override read/write behavior; pytest and pre-commit passed on touched files.
[specifics: app: fleet-platform-app; postgres_table: eventgrid_filter_overrides; migration: 034; PgStore_methods: resolve_eventgrid_filter_override, upsert_eventgrid_filter_override; override_precedence: subscription > customer > global default; files_modified: functions/activities/eventgrid.py, functions/triggers/http_eventgrid.py; ARM_template_support: eventTypes parameter supported in ARM event subscription template; token_rotation_preserve: eventTypes and advancedFilters preserved during token rotation; tests_added: unit tests for Event Grid helper override application and PgStore override read/write behavior; ci_checks: pytest and pre-commit passed on touched files]"]}
